title: Multiple Discussion Chains + Textarea Input
items: Stack unlimited discussions: click + Discussion as many times as you want, each gets its own purple banner with a Close button and dot on the trunk
Input upgraded from 80-char single line to a resizable textarea (500 char limit) — write full thoughts while waiting on processes
Each discussion is independently closeable with undo toast. Old single-discussion data auto-migrates to the new format
Enter submits, Shift+Enter for newlines, Escape cancels

title: Undo Toast on Action Queue + Discussion Close
items: Clicking Done on action queue banner now shows 5-second undo toast instead of instant commit — prevents accidental confirmations
Close Discussion button now shows undo toast with discussion topic — can reopen within 5 seconds if clicked by mistake
Dismiss on action queue skips undo (intentional action, no safety net needed)

title: Backlog Rendering — Dynamic Groups
items: Fixed Backlog section not showing items — was hardcoded to look for Now/Soon/Later groups but actual data uses Pipeline, AI, Architecture, Services, etc.
Groups now render dynamically from whatever group names exist in the data, sorted alphabetically with color-coded labels
Each item shows its group badge, status badge, and full description on hover

title: Discussion Chains + PQ Auto-Sync
items: Discussion Chain: click + Discussion to pause workflow and start a discussion. Purple dot appears on the trunk with topic label. Close it when done and workflow resumes where you left off

PQ status auto-sync: workflow state now drives PQ item statuses automatically — coding starts marks items In Progress, checkpoint deploys mark Deployed, completion marks Done. No more manual status migrations
Discussion button disables when a discussion is already open. All buttons show contextual disabled tooltips

title: Workflow Dashboard — Smart Button States
items: Action buttons now auto-enable/disable based on workflow state: +Checkpoint requires coding to be in-progress, +Completion requires at least one checkpoint and no existing completion branch, New Story requires branches to archive
Disabled buttons show at 35% opacity with contextual tooltips explaining why they're disabled (e.g. 'Start coding first — nothing to checkpoint yet')
Button states update automatically on every render cycle including auto-refresh polling

title: Workflow Dashboard — Meaningful Tooltips + Collapsible Backlog
items: Tooltips now explain what each node actually means instead of generic 'Click to cycle LED' — hover any node to understand the process
Backlog section added to Workflow Dashboard — collapsed by default, click to expand. Shows items grouped by priority (Now/Soon/Later) with status badges
All workflow data (PQ, Backlog, Active Story, History) now visible in one page — no tab-hopping needed

title: Action Queue — Interactive Workflow Dashboard
items: Action Queue banner: Claude writes action requests to KV, the Workflow Dashboard shows them as interactive banners with buttons — no more typing 'DEPLOY COMPLETE' back and forth
Supports commit_push (with Copy Commit Msg button), deploy_confirm, test_request (Pass/Fail), question (custom options), and info action types
Dashboard polls KV every 10 seconds and picks up new actions automatically. Christian clicks a button, response writes back to KV, Claude reads it
New Story button restyled: dark fill background instead of invisible transparent-on-black

title: GUI Workflow — Tooltips, Auto-Refresh, State Seed
items: Tooltips on all interactive elements: hover any button, SVG node, delete button, or arrowhead to see what it does
Auto-refresh polling every 10 seconds — workflow diagram updates live without needing F5, perfect for keeping on a second monitor
State seed: first load after reset now shows the correct current position (trunk progress, completed checkpoint branches) instead of blank

title: GUI Workflow UX — Undo Toast + Nav Fix
items: Branch delete now instant with 5-second Undo toast bar instead of confirm dialog — faster workflow, forgiving mistakes
ADMIN nav link on workflow.html matched to other pages: 10px font, orange border-left separator, consistent style

title: Workflow Dashboard — Branch Collapsing + Row Wrapping
items: Completed branches (all nodes green) collapse to a small green checkmark dot on the trunk with version label — reclaims horizontal space automatically
Row wrapping: when the diagram exceeds container width, trunk wraps to a new row below with a dashed connector arc. No horizontal scrolling needed
Collapsed branches are clickable to delete. Expanded branches retain the × delete button
Dynamic SVG height adjusts based on number of rows and whether any branches are expanded

title: Workflow Dashboard Polish + ADMIN Nav Fix
items: SVG arrowhead spear at end of trunk line for visual polish
Delete button (×) on checkpoint/completion branches — hover turns red, click to remove with confirmation
T-120: ADMIN nav link now clickable from all admin sub-pages (Tasks, Feedback, Tester, Changelog, Diagrams) — links back to admin.html hub
Backlog: Quick navigation between Tasks, Feedback, and Workflow pages

title: Workflow Dashboard SVG Rebuild — Branch/Merge Visualization
items: Complete SVG rebuild of workflow.html — replaces linear div-based pipeline with dynamic branch/merge visualization inspired by git branch diagrams
Main trunk (Level 1): Start → Scan → Coding with Story Done endpoint. Checkpoint branches (Level 2) fork off coding with 5 nodes each, merge back to trunk
Completion branches extend with Testing → Done nodes before merging. Visual distinction: blue strokes for completion, orange for checkpoints
Click any node circle to cycle red → yellow → green. +Checkpoint, +Completion, New Story, and Reset All buttons
Auto-detection from PQ data: in-progress items light Coding, deployed items light branch nodes. State persists to opsflow_workflow_state in localStorage/KV

title: Workflow Dashboard — LED Pipeline for Dev Cycle Tracking
items: New Admin page: workflow.html — 9-step LED pipeline (Scan → Coding → Commit Ready → Committed → Pushed → Deployed → Verify → Testing → Done)
Click any LED to cycle red/yellow/green. Each step labeled with who does it (Claude, Christian, or Auto)
Checkpoint vs Completion toggle — disables Testing and Done steps for checkpoint deploys
Auto-detection: PQ in-progress items auto-set Coding LED, deployed items auto-set Deployed LED, testing items auto-set Testing LED
Current PQ display, cycle history log, Reset Cycle button. State persists to localStorage/KV

title: Auto-detect KV Failures — Services Page LED Updates Automatically
items: opsflow-sync.js now detects KV write failures (daily limit, network, API outage) and automatically flips the Cloudflare service LED to red on the Services page
KV write success flips the LED back to green. Covers OpsFlow.set(), setVersion(), kvBulk(), and kvRequest()
Workflow doc updated: Checkpoint deploy now includes PQ/Backlog/Feedback housekeeping
Feedback triaged: Pipeline layout scroll issue (UX) and Workflow Dashboard proposal (Admin) added to Backlog

title: Editable Card Infrastructure — Click-to-Edit Table Values
items: Card table value cells are now click-to-edit: hover shows pencil icon, click opens textarea with Save/Cancel, Escape cancels. Works on all cards across all phases
User-edited values persist to localStorage via new state.cardData structure. Edited cells show blue left-border indicator
Edit lock respected: cells are read-only when card LED is green (completed). Undo Completion reverts to yellow and re-enables editing
Foundation for P1.1 through G1 GREEN status: editable tables + existing interactive checklists + existing Mark as Complete = full card lifecycle

title: Rebuild P1 Card Content — Templates + 45 RPM Adaptor Product Data
items: Rebuilt all P1 templateDetailContent (P1.1–G1) and productDetailContent for 45 RPM Adaptor to match new card definitions from design review
Templates: New purpose statements, field structures, checklists, and tips aligned to P2M Flow. P1.4 factors renamed, P1.5 adds per-model sub-card concept, G1 adds CONDITIONAL GO option

45 RPM Adaptor: All real Helium 10/market data preserved but restructured. P1.3 adds 3-model product line (EasyLift, PinchGrip, The Crown). P1.4 rescored to 25/30. P1.5 includes per-model print time estimates
Application Design Document (v1.2) and P1 Card Review summaries added to docs

title: Align Phase 1 + Phase 2 Card Descriptions to P2M Flow Master
items: P1.1–P1.5 and G1 step definitions updated to match Flow diagram: richer descriptions, context questions, named scoring factors, production feasibility language
P2.1: CAD Design → Design Brief / CAD Spec (adds spec + compliance scope). P2.3: Lock Print Settings → Lock Settings + BOM. G2 gate criteria expanded to match Flow
Phase 2 card titles and outputs now reflect full BOM, QC criteria, and compliance language

title: Simplify Nav — Admin Hub Is Single Entry Point
items: Removed Changelog, Tasks, Feedback, Tester links from top nav on all 10 pages
Nav now: Pipeline | Media Pipeline | Workshop | Services | ADMIN
Admin pages show ADMIN as current with orange breadcrumb (Admin > PageName) linking back to admin.html

title: Deployed / Testing / Rework Status Badges
items: 3 new status badges: Deployed (purple), Testing (yellow pulse), Rework (red pulse)
Updated status lifecycle: In Progress → Deployed → Testing → Rework → In Progress
Workflow doc expanded: status lifecycle, who-sets-what table, group batch workflow, commit format

title: Admin Section Complete — Landing Page + Diagrams
items: Admin landing page (admin.html): Card-based hub linking to Tasks, Changelog, Tester, Feedback, and Diagrams. Reference section with Lookup Data placeholder
Diagrams page (diagrams.html): 3 live diagrams (P2M Complete Flow, Change Process Flow, Cloudflare Architecture) and 5 planned architecture diagram placeholders. Breadcrumb nav back to Admin
ADMIN nav label now links to admin.html across all 10 P2M pages. P2M Complete Flow diagram added to repo. Working folder reorganized

title: Admin Nav Split
items: Nav bar now visually separates User pages (Pipeline, Media, Workshop, Services) from Admin pages (Changelog, Tasks, Feedback, Tester) with an orange ADMIN label divider
CSS-only change across all 8 P2M pages. No page moves, just clear visual grouping. Lays groundwork for Admin landing page and diagrams page
Backlog reorganized: 80 items, Admin restructure tasks added, P2M pipeline build-out tasks (P4-P7 cards, channel support, dependency system) queued in phase order

title: Backlog Group Tags & Filter
items: Group tags: All 73 PQ and Backlog items categorized into 12 groups (AI, Admin, Architecture, Infrastructure, Keywords, Media, Pipeline, QA, Services, Templates, UX, Workshop). Colored group pills displayed on every task card
Filter dropdown: Backlog section now has a Group filter with per-group item counts, Clear Filter button, and 'Showing X of Y' counter. Quickly scan related items without scrolling through 68+
Migration v0.87.0: Auto-assigns groups by title pattern matching on first load. Existing task data is preserved

title: Status Badge Reset & Workshop Complete
items: Status Reset button: hover any non-New status badge to reveal a reset arrow that sends it back to New. Works on PQ and Backlog items. Fixes one-way status trap where Discussing/Done couldn't be reversed
T-036 (Workshop data review) and T-041 (Workshop review & process update) marked Done. All 6 Workshop phases GREEN, 100% completion, data flowing to Pipeline COGS
T-095 (AI Assist Prompt R&D) formalized in workflow doc. Fixed vv display bug in v0.85.0 rework

title: User Test Item in Tester
items: User Test section (T-093): Every Tester config now includes a 'User Test' section with a manual verification item. Christian can always PASS/FAIL and annotate findings, even when all automated test items pass
Workflow doc updated: User Test item is now a standard part of every tester config seed

title: Complete Testing Button — 2-State Enable/Disable
items: Complete Testing button (T-054): Button now starts disabled and shows progress ('X of Y tested') until all test items have a result (PASS, FAIL, or SKIP). Only enables when 100% coverage is reached
REWORK state: Button shows FAIL count and stays disabled until all FAILs are resolved
T-014 (Labor Roster) set to Done. T-057/T-058 (W1.2/W1.3 trim) archived as decided to keep

title: Labor Roster — Repeatable Cards for Phase 5
items: Labor Roster (T-014): Replaced static W5.1 (Owner) and W5.2 (Additional Labor) cards with a dynamic, repeatable Labor Roster in Workshop Phase 5. Same pattern as Printer Fleet and Materials Library — add, edit, verify, remove labor entries
Each labor entry captures: Name, Role (dropdown: Owner/Operator, Employee, Contractor, Helper), Hourly Rate, Labor in Pricing flag (Absorbed vs Include in COGS), Hours/Day, Status (Active/Inactive)
Pipeline COGS bridge (P3.1) updated to loop through active labor roster entries instead of reading single static fields. Shows absorbed vs costed labor with rate summary
Data migration preserves existing W5.1 data (Christian Caspers, Owner/Operator, Absorbed, 4 hrs/day) as first roster entry. W5.2 backlog item auto-completed as merged

title: HTML Entity Sweep Suite-Wide
items: HTML Entity Sweep (T-088): Replaced HTML entities (— & × • → … ’ ↑) with Unicode equivalents in all JavaScript strings across 8 files. Prevents double-escaping when strings pass through escHtml() or innerHTML assignment

Affected pages: Workshop (hint text, purpose strings, buttons), Services (descriptions, category labels), Pipeline (upload UI), Media Center (status arrows, ellipsis), Feedback, Tasks, Changelog, LaunchPad
Removed & decode workaround in services.html edit form (no longer needed with clean source data)
Workflow doc updated: new rule to embed workflow actions in task plans, updated HTML entity gotcha for JS strings vs raw HTML

title: KV Cache Architecture & Data-Driven Pages
items: Full KV Cache Layer (T-089): New OpsFlow.get(key) and OpsFlow.set(key, data) API with stale detection. KV is source of truth, localStorage is cache. Stale threshold of 5 minutes triggers KV refresh. Graceful degradation when KV unreachable

Data-Driven Changelog: Timeline rendered entirely from JSON data (localStorage/KV/static file). Changelog HTML reduced from 2275 lines to ~290 lines. New shared/changelog-data.json as seed file. Zero hardcoded timeline entries in HTML
Data-Driven Tester Config: TEST_VERSION and TEST_SECTIONS loaded from opsflow_tester_config (localStorage/KV). Tester HTML no longer needs version-specific edits on each version bump

All P2M Pages Migrated: Every page converted from direct localStorage.getItem/setItem to OpsFlow.getLocal()/OpsFlow.set(). Unified data access pattern across Pipeline, Workshop, Services, Media, Tasks, Feedback, Tester, LaunchPad
SYNC_KEYS Audit & Fix: Corrected mismatches between SYNC_KEYS and actual localStorage key names (opsflow_tasks vs opsflow_tasks_v2, opsflow_workshop vs workshopState_v2). All keys now match reality
Script Load Order: opsflow-sync.js moved before main script blocks on all 9 pages so OpsFlow API is available during initialization

Task Status Lifecycle & Archive (T-091): Status cycle updated to New → Discussing → In Progress → Done (terminal, no wrap). Archive button appears on Done items — click to move to Recently Completed with timestamp. Claude auto-updates statuses during workflow steps

Lessons Learned Workflow Step (T-090): After each version’s testing completes, Claude delivers a brief retro summary before reviewing the PQ — covering what shipped, what went well, improvements, and patterns discovered

Tester REWORK Status (T-087): When any test item is marked FAIL, the HALT banner enters amber REWORK state with pulsing animation. Complete Testing button is disabled and shows FAIL count. Alert blocks completion until all FAILs are flipped to PASS. Prevents premature test completion with unresolved failures

Feedback Section Removed from Tester (T-071): Feedback form (category, priority, text input) removed from Tester page — now a standalone page at feedback.html. Tester page refocused on testing workflow only. Page title updated from “Test & Feedback” to “Tester”

W1.1 Electricity Rate Instructions (T-013): Collapsible “How to calculate this value” section with step-by-step guide for deriving per-kWh rate from a utility bill. Covers energy charges, tiered rates, time-of-use pricing, and sanity-check range
Fleet Capacity Summary (T-009): Phase 2 now shows total productive hours/day and hours/week across all active printers below the fleet cards. Auto-updates when fleet changes

PQ Cleanup: 6 completed items (T-004, T-005, T-006, T-007, T-008, T-012) moved to Recently Completed. T-009 split into done (verification) and remaining (capacity calc). Process review backlog items added

Materials Library Restructure (T-005): Simplified from primary/backup supplier model to one filament type + one supplier per card. Removed supplierPrimary, costPrimary, supplierBackup, costBackup fields. Single supplier and costKg fields. COGS calculation simplified
AI Assist Updated: Materials prompt fields updated to match new single-supplier card structure
Data Migration: Existing materials with old primary/backup fields reset to clean default (PETG-HF). User re-enters fresh cards with simplified structure
New Backlog (SOON): P3.1 Material Bill per Product, P3.1 COGS Auto-Calculate from Materials Library — future integration between Materials Library and Pipeline COGS

Cloudflare KV Data Store (T-085/T-086): Extended existing Worker with KV namespace binding. New API endpoints: GET/PUT/DELETE /kv/:key, GET /kv-list, POST /kv-bulk. All authenticated via X-Upload-Secret. Stepping stone to full database migration

Shared Sync Helper (opsflow-sync.js): New shared JS module loaded on every page. Auto-sets version from KV, backs up localStorage to KV on page unload, auto-restores from KV if localStorage is wiped. Recovery mode for browser data loss
Dynamic Version Display: All 9 pages now use <span class="suite-ver"> for version numbers, updated dynamically by opsflow-sync.js from KV. Eliminates need to manually edit version strings in HTML

Architecture Diagram Backlog: Added 6 sub-items under existing architecture diagrams parent task: P2M Pipeline, Workshop Calc Engine, Suite Topology, Data Storage & Sync, Image Pipeline, Services Flow
Secret Rotation: Rotated UPLOAD_SECRET and REMOVEBG_API_KEY to fresh GUIDs

LaunchPad Admin Mode (T-083): Gear icon in top bar toggles admin mode. Add, edit, and delete sphere shortcuts via modal form. Create new groups and rename existing ones by clicking the group label. All changes save to localStorage
Rework: “New” status badge color changed from invisible dark grey to visible light grey (#c9d1d9) so it actually shows on dark background
Unique Task IDs: Every PQ and Backlog item now has a permanent ID (T-001, T-002, etc.) that never changes regardless of sort order or position. Displayed on each task card for easy reference
Status LEDs: Task status cycles through 4 states: New (gray) → Discussing (yellow) → In Progress (blue, pulsing) → Done (green). Click to cycle. Both user and Claude can update
Primary/Backup Suppliers: Materials Library now has separate Primary and Backup Supplier fields with individual pricing. COGS always uses the higher of the two prices for conservative costing
Migration: Existing tasks auto-assigned IDs. Old “working” status migrated to “In Progress”. Old single supplier field preserved via costKg fallback
Card Reference Labels: Materials Library cards now show ML.1, ML.2, etc. Software & Tools cards show ST.1, ST.2, etc. Fleet already had PF.1, PF.2 — all dynamic cards now have shorthand reference IDs
Detail panel headers: Opening any dynamic card shows its reference (e.g. “ML.1 — PETG-HF”) for quick identification
AI Prompt references: Fleet and material AI prompts now include the card reference (PF.x / ML.x) for context
W3.5 Removed: Packaging Defaults removed from Workshop Setup — packaging is product-specific and belongs in the Pipeline (P4.1 Packaging Design), not as a workshop-level default
Pipeline bridge updated: P3.1 COGS bridge no longer pulls packaging from Workshop — references P4.1 instead. P3.2 Pricing Strategy bridge cleaned up
Migration: Existing localStorage data cleaned up automatically — W3.5 state removed on load

Machine Economics AI Assist: AI Assist on W3.1 generates a smart prompt from your fleet composition (models, prices, counts) and fills both W3.1 lifespan and W3.2 maintenance budget + hours/year in one paste
Card Locking: W3.1/W3.2 locked until all fleet cards are green, W3.3 locked until W1.1 + fleet verified, W3.4 locked until W3.1–W3.3 verified — enforces correct data entry order
Enhanced hints: Improved field hints on W3.1 (lifespan ranges), W3.2 (utilization guidance), W3.5 (packaging cost sources), with contextual help for new users facing blank fields

Clear AI Fields: New button appears on fleet and materials cards when all AI-fillable fields already have values — one click clears the fields, regenerates the prompt, and re-opens the card ready for a fresh AI lookup
Materials AI Assist: Materials Library cards now have AI Assist — enter material name, get prompt for type, cost/kg, and popular suppliers
Apply button gate: Apply button starts disabled across all AI Assist panels, enables only when paste field has content
Engine refactor: createClaudePromptConfig() now accepts introLine parameter — fully card-agnostic with getFleetIntro() and getMaterialIntro() helpers
Unified watcher: attachDynListeners AI Assist block handles both fleet and materials with card-specific config lookup
Brand field: New “Brand” field between Printer Name and Model — separates manufacturer from model number for cleaner data structure
AI Assist updated: Prompt now composes “I have a [Brand] [Model] 3D printer” using both fields; model removed from AI-fillable fields since user enters brand/model before AI Assist activates
Migration: Existing fleet data auto-splits combined model values (e.g., “Bambu Lab X1C” → brand: “Bambu Lab”, model: “X1C”) with smart lookup map and generic fallback
Vendor-neutral hint: AI Assist disabled hint updated to “enter brand & model to enable”
Model gate: AI Assist toggle disabled on new printers with hint “enter printer model to enable” — prevents dead-end prompts with placeholder text
Auto-expand: When user enters a model and tabs out, AI Assist auto-expands with prompt pre-generated and ready to copy
Model lookup list: Brand/model names persist to localStorage as users add printers — seeds from existing fleet on page load, future: dropdown typeahead
Refactored bindings: Copy and Apply button handlers extracted to reusable bindAiCopyBtn() and bindAiApplyBtn() functions

AI Assist (beta): Fleet card detail panels now include a collapsible AI Assist section — generates a smart Claude prompt based on known values, user copies to Claude, pastes response, and fields auto-fill
Reusable engine: createClaudePromptConfig() accepts field definitions and current values, returns prompt + parser — designed for Suite-wide reuse
Response parser: Handles case-insensitive key:value pairs, numeric extraction, build volume normalization (256x256x256 → 256 x 256 x 256), and shows fill results
Build Volume rename: “Build Plate (mm)” → “Build Volume (mm)” with X×Y×Z format; migration adds Z dimension to existing 2D data
Backlog: Template Builder (Suite-wide template definitions) and Forms Builder (dynamic form generation) added as Later-priority items
PF.X numbering: Fleet printer cards now display PF.1, PF.2, PF.3, etc. based on array position — auto-renumbers when printers are added or removed
Card display reorg: step-number shows PF.X, title shows printer name, description shows model, output shows status
Detail panel header: Shows “PF.X — Printer Name” format (e.g., PF.1 — Galileo Seven)
Data review: Confirmed all fleet fields (price, watts, status, hours, name, model, speed, plate) are sufficient for P2M pipeline flow
Feedback triage: 2 items from v0.61.2 and v0.67.1 moved to Backlog (product template, services indicator)
W1.3 linked fields: Service Subscriptions and Breakdown auto-pull from Services page — filters to production, sales, and operations categories only (manufacturing costs, not general infrastructure)
W1.3 Total Monthly: Auto-calculated Internet + Service Subscriptions with full breakdown
remove.bg updated: Changed from credits ($0.20/credit) to Lite subscription ($8.10/mo) on Services page
Helium 10 fix: Corrected HTML default from $39/mo to FREE (trial)
Hover.com added: Domain registration ($15/yr) added to Services under Infrastructure
Backlog: Added AI product image tool evaluation and user-driven cost line items
Payment Structure dropdown: “How do you pay for your space?” — Mortgage with Escrow, Mortgage without Escrow, Rent, Own Outright — controls which cost fields are visible or disabled
disableWhen system: New conditional field disable (parallel to showWhen hide) — greyed-out fields with “Included in your escrow amount” hint
Escrow field: Monthly Escrow ($/mo) visible only for Mortgage with Escrow — bundles property tax & insurance
Rent & Own Outright: Rent shows Monthly Rent + Renter Insurance; Own Outright shows Property Tax + Insurance + Maintenance only
Allocated Cost: Calculation branches by payment structure — correct sum for each scenario
W1.2 Calculation Method: Dropdown with Dedicated Business Space, US Home Office, and UK Home Office options — adapts visible fields based on selection
Cost breakdown: Replaced single “Monthly Cost” with Mortgage/Rent, Property Tax, Insurance, and Maintenance sub-fields
Auto-calculated: Business-Use % and Allocated Monthly Cost computed from square footage ratio and method selection
Conditional fields: Total Home Sq Ft hides when Dedicated Business Space is selected (100% allocation)
Status tracking: Clickable “Working” badge on PQ and Backlog items — pulsing green dot shows active task at a glance
Edit/Save: Inline editing for task titles and descriptions with pencil button, Save/Cancel controls, and Escape to cancel. Edit disabled on Working PQ items.
Promote to Backlog: “↑ Backlog” button on Feedback items (both Feedback and Tester pages) — maps category & priority, adds to Backlog, removes from Feedback
Feedback triage: Processed 3 user feedback items into Backlog — task status tracking, inline edit/save, feedback-to-backlog promotion
Migrations: Injected 3 new Backlog items and cleared processed feedback from localStorage
Workflow doc: Fixed “all 7 pages” → “all 8 pages” in Step 2
Feedback page: New standalone page at feedback.html — submit ideas, bugs, and notes without navigating to the Tester
Nav bar updated: “Feedback” link added between Tasks and Tester on all 8 P2M pages
Shared storage: Feedback form on both pages reads/writes the same localStorage key — items appear in both places
W1.1 completed: Moved [WS] W1.1 Electricity Rate from Priority Queue to Recently Completed (51 total)
HTML PQ sync: Priority Queue HTML updated to match live localStorage state (W3.1 now Up Next)
Feedback-first rule: Added Step 0 to workflow — Claude reads Tester feedback before starting any work
Tester version fix: HTML source versionTag corrected from stale v0.58.0
Backlog expansion: Added 31 new backlog items across 4 categories — [WS] Workshop (13), [SVC] Services (2), [KW] Keywords Everywhere (4), [AI] Claude Integration (12)
Processing type tags: Each backlog item tagged with (MANUAL), (ASSIST), or (AUTOMATE) in description for easy scanning and prioritization
W1.1 verified: Electricity Rate ($0.107/kWh) confirmed from Duke Energy bill and marked Green in Workshop. HTML default state updated as safety net.
Workflow doc: Added Keywords Everywhere API reference, Claude Integration Plan summary with automation levels, and updated current state

title: Backlog Delete Action
items: X delete button: Every Backlog card now has a red × button to remove unwanted items. Positioned before the Promote button for quick access
Confirm dialog: Browser confirm() prevents accidental deletes — shows item title so you know exactly what you’re removing
Auto-cleanup: The “Add X delete action” backlog item removed itself via migration — first completed Up Next item from the Priority Queue

title: Sort Fix & Backlog Corrections
items: Sort bug fixed: priOrder mapped high to 0 (falsy in JS) — 0 || 1 made Now items sort identically to Soon. Changed to { high:1, medium:2, low:3 } so all values are truthy
Debounce confirmed: 2-second deferred re-sort now works correctly for all priority transitions (Now ↔ Soon ↔ Later)
Backlog corrected: “Complete Testing” item updated to 2-state enable/disable spec matching user’s intent (disabled during TESTING IN PROGRESS, enabled when all items tested)
Architecture diagrams: New backlog item for building interactive arch diagrams across the Suite (Later priority)

title: Priority LED UX & Feedback Pipeline
items: Debounced re-sort: Priority LED click-to-cycle now updates in place — list re-sorts after 2 seconds of no clicks instead of immediately, preventing items from jumping away from cursor
PQ LEDs stable: Priority Queue LED cycling unchanged (PQ doesn’t sort by priority), but benefits from consistent UX pattern
Feedback → Backlog: 2 v0.64.6 feedback items triaged into Backlog — “X delete on Backlog items” (Soon) and “Complete Testing disable” (Later) — auto-cleared from feedback via migration
Dev self-testing: Added pre-handoff verification step to dev workflow — Claude visually checks live site before handing off to tester

title: Backlog Sort & Clickable Priority LEDs
items: Auto-sort: Backlog renders in priority order — Now (high) first, Soon (medium) middle, Later (low) last — sorted on every render and persisted
Click-to-cycle: Click any Backlog priority LED to cycle Now → Soon → Later → Now. Re-sorts automatically after change
HTML defaults sorted: Static HTML backlog reordered with Now/Soon/Later section comments for clean fallback seeding
Feedback processed: 2 improvement items (sort request + LED click request) addressed by this version’s code, auto-cleared via migration

title: localStorage Migrations
items: Migration system: Tasks page now runs keyed migrations on load — injects new backlog items and clears processed feedback directly into localStorage without requiring manual resets
First migrations: Workshop review (Now) and Priority LED feature (Now) injected into Backlog; Workshop feedback item auto-cleared from tester feedback
Migration tracking: Each migration runs once per browser via opsflow_tasks_migrations key — safe to re-run, idempotent, won’t duplicate items

title: Priority LED Labels & Feedback Pipeline
items: Priority LEDs: Backlog and PQ items now display Now/Soon/Later text labels next to their priority dot — high = Now (red), medium = Soon (orange), low = Later (blue)
Feedback → Backlog pipeline: Tester feedback items are triaged into Backlog at priority-appropriate positions (Now = top, Soon = middle, Later = bottom), then removed from feedback
New backlog items: Workshop review & process update (Now), Priority LED manual editing feature (Now) — 27 total backlog items
Workflow improvement: Established formal process — feedback drives tasks, tasks drive work, user controls promotion and sequencing

title: Tester Auto-Reset
items: Auto-reset: Tester page generates a fingerprint from TEST_SECTIONS content; when sections change for the same version, test state and HALT auto-clear on page load
Fresh tests guaranteed: No more manual “Reset Current Version” needed after dev pushes updated test items
Feedback preserved: Auto-reset only clears test results and HALT — submitted feedback items are never lost
Changelog backfill: Added missing v0.64.1 and v0.64.2 entries, updated Milestones 3 (90%) and 4 (38%), bumped Versions Shipped to 78

title: LED First-Click Visual Sync
items: First-click fix: cycleLED now reads the displayed auto-computed status (autoStatus) instead of the stored status, so the first click always produces a visible change
Visual consistency: Eliminates the “phantom click” where the first LED click appeared to do nothing because stored and displayed status were out of sync

title: LED Date Advance & Toast Notification
items: Date advance fix: Cycling LED to green now always pushes the renewal date forward by one billing cycle, then catches up if still past due
Toast notification: When cycling a LED to green on a service without a renewal period set, a toast guides the user to configure Monthly/Yearly in the edit modal
Version bump: All P2M pages updated to v0.64.1

title: Interactive Tasks & LED Cycle
items: Promote: Backlog items now have “↑ Promote” button to move them into the Priority Queue
Demote: PQ items have “↓ Backlog” button to send them back to Backlog
Reorder: PQ items have position dropdown to change their order in the queue
Persistence: Task state stored in localStorage (opsflow_tasks_v2), seeds from static HTML on first load
LED click-to-cycle: Click any service LED to cycle green → yellow → red → green
Auto-advance renewal: When cycling a service LED back to green, renewal date auto-advances by billing cycle (monthly/yearly)
Processed 3 v0.63.0 feedback items (all addressed by this version’s interactive tasks feature)

title: Services & Integrations
items: New page: Services & Integrations dashboard — third-party service tracker with LED status indicators
7 services: remove.bg, Helium 10, Amazon Seller Central, Cloudflare, Onshape, Monday.com, Calendly
Service cards: at-a-glance cost, credits, renewal date, billing type, and status LED per service
Smart status: LED auto-computes from renewal proximity and credit levels (green/yellow/red)
Management: add, edit, and delete services via modal — data persisted in localStorage
Categories: services grouped by Production Tools, Sales & Marketing, Operations, Infrastructure
LaunchPad: S&I sphere activated (was “UP NEXT”, now LIVE with purple glow)
Navigation: “Services” link added to P2M nav bar across all pages
Promoted 5 remaining feedback items to Backlog with priorities (19 total)
Feedback → Backlog flow established: promote or remove, no lingering items

title: Readability & Feedback Cleanup
items: Grey text brightened: all #484f58 meta text upgraded to #8b949e for readability (recurring accessibility feedback)
Layout fix: Complete Testing bar moved above Feedback section per user request
Clear All Feedback: confirmation dialog now shows item count and detailed warning
Feedback audit: cleaned 20 completed/duplicate items from localStorage (30 → 10 remaining)
Backlog: added “Monday.com-style Workshop task manager” from v0.61.0 feedback

title: Always-On Feedback
items: Feedback decoupled from test sessions: submit ideas, bugs, or notes anytime without starting a test
Feedback no longer triggers HALT state — only PASS/FAIL/SKIP test interactions do
New storage format: flat array with version tags per item (migrates old format automatically)
Feedback items now display newest first with date + version in meta line
“Reset Current Version” no longer clears feedback — test data and feedback are independent
Separated admin actions: “Clear Test History” and “Clear All Feedback” as distinct buttons
Resolved localStorage defaults sync (Priority Queue #1) — already handled by build process

title: Tester HALT Indicator & Priority LEDs
items: HALT banner: RED pulsing LED when testing is in progress (“Do Not Modify”), GREEN when complete (“Ready for Dev”), grey when idle
HALT state triggers automatically on any PASS/FAIL/SKIP click or feedback submission
HALT state persists in localStorage per version — survives page refresh
Priority LED dropdown: Now (red), Soon (yellow), Later (blue) — defaults to Soon
Feedback items display colored LED dot + priority label in meta line
Backlog: added “Reduce repetitive manual testing” from v0.59.0 feedback

title: Card-Based LaunchPad Redesign
items: Card groups: Tools & Personal sections now render as individual cards that dynamically flow via CSS Grid
Layout widened from 1000px to 1400px — uses full viewport width
Personal groups split into separate cards: Trading, Banking, Shopping, AI & Media
Amazon moved from Finance to Shopping group (it’s shopping, not banking)
Colored ground shadows: each sphere now casts a glow matching its color via CSS --glow variable
Grey text standardized with CSS custom properties: --text-dim (#8b949e), --text-primary (#c9d1d9), --text-bright (#e6edf3)
Responsive card grid: auto-fill with minmax(280px, 1fr) — flows on desktop, tablet, and mobile
Glow map in JS propagates --glow to sphere-item level for both hover glow and ground shadow

title: LaunchPad Tester Feedback & Layout Polish
items: Bug fix: Capsule pills were collapsed — replaced two-div approach with split gradient on single element
S&I sphere label text brightened — opacity now only on sphere, not the label
Ground shadows reworked to subtle light reflection (dark-on-dark was invisible)
Version number added to LaunchPad upper-right corner
Layout tightened: reduced spacing, padding, and margins across all sections
Personal spheres grouped by category: Trading, Finance, AI & Media
Removed BG Removal carry-forward test (irrelevant to LaunchPad changes)
Backlog: Tasks/Tester root-level migration, favicon icons on spheres

title: LaunchPad Polish & Link Expansion
items: Bug fix: GitHub sphere URL corrected from ccasperslocal to ccaspers2026
Added 8 new sphere links from Chrome homepage shortcuts
Tools: Seller Central, OnShape, Monday, Calendly added
Personal: TradingView, TraderSync, Discord, Claude added
Capsule pills reworked: full page names centered over two-tone pill (no more split words)
Ground shadows improved: higher opacity, blur filter, wider spread for better visibility
Tasks page updated with v0.56.0 tester feedback items in priority queue

title: LaunchPad & Suite Navigation
items: New LaunchPad page at root level (/launchpad.html) — future Chrome homepage
3D sphere links with radial gradients, inset shadows, specular highlights, and ground shadows
Three sections: Suite (P2M, Services & Integrations), Tools & Services (Helium 10, remove.bg, Cloudflare, GitHub), Personal (tastytrade, Truist, OptionStrat, YouTube, Amazon, ChatGPT)
Tools & Personal sections are localStorage-driven (opsflow_launchpad) — add/remove/change links without pushing code
P2M sub-links panel below Suite spheres for quick navigation into P2M pages
Time-based greeting (Good morning/afternoon/evening)
Hover effect: scale up 12%, colored glow, ground shadow spreads
OpsFlowBetter brand link in all P2M nav bars now routes to /launchpad.html
Cleaned up premature dock bar CSS from P2M shared component block

title: Shared Nav+Header Component & Professional Logo
items: Created “P2M Shared Components” CSS block — identical on all 6 pages (nav + header)
New .p2m-header class: h1 22px/600/#fff, version 13px/#58a6ff, tagline 12px/#8b949e
All inline header styles eliminated — every page uses CSS classes only
New upward-trending flow arrow SVG logo replaces old “OFB” text-in-a-box
Logo: blue rounded square with white rising trend arrow (24x24)
Nav .suite-home uses display:flex; align-items:center for clean logo alignment

title: Standardized Headers & OFB Logo
items: All 6 page headers now follow same pattern: Title + Version tag (blue) + Tagline (gray)
Media Pipeline: header and controls split into separate bars
OFB logo icon (blue rounded square with “OFB”) replaces “← Suite Home” text in nav
Hovering OFB logo shows “OpsFlowBetter Suite Home” tooltip

title: P2M-Only Nav & Suite Home
items: Removed “Our Products” and “OpsFlowBetter” from nav — non-P2M pages don’t belong in P2M nav
Added “← Suite Home” link in upper-left corner of nav bar to return to opsflowbetter.com
P2M links centered in nav: Pipeline | Media Pipeline | Workshop | Changelog | Tasks | Tester
Architecture rule: nav only shows pages within its own application (subdomain/subfolder scoping)

title: Fixed Nav Bar, Uniform Colors & Auto-Toggle OFF
items: Suite nav is now position: fixed at the top of every page — centered, same height, same pixels across all 6 pages
Body padding-top: 36px on all pages so content doesn’t hide behind the nav
All nav links are uniform blue (#58a6ff) — no more per-link rainbow colors
Current page shown as white bold text (not a link)
BG Removal toggle auto-flips to OFF after each successful credit use to prevent accidental burns

title: Standardized Nav, Inline Queue Buttons & localStorage Data Layer
items: Suite nav (.suite-nav) standardized across all 6 pages — same class, 12px font, flex layout, pipe separators, current-page highlight
Queue action buttons (X + Process/Retry) now display inline via flexbox instead of stacking vertically
Tasks page reads from opsflow_tasks localStorage if present — allows remote updates without code deploys
Tester page loads TEST_VERSION & TEST_SECTIONS from opsflow_tester_config localStorage if present
Static HTML remains as fallback when no localStorage data exists

title: “Image Pipeline” → “Media Pipeline”
items: Renamed “Image Pipeline” to “Media Pipeline” across all 6 pages — future-proofs for video support
Nav links, page titles, and topbar labels all updated
Tasks page: Priority #1 completed, queue renumbered
Tasks recently-completed list updated (25 items)

title: BG Removal Toggle, Image Pipeline Rename & Gallery Dedup
items: BG Removal ON/OFF toggle in top bar — defaults to OFF so no credits are used on upload. Turn ON when ready to crop. Persists in localStorage.
When toggle is OFF, uploads save to cloud storage only — no credits consumed, no auto-processing
Process/Retry buttons blocked when toggle is OFF with clear toast message
Credit-related errors now show “Cropping bypassed — add credits and retry to crop” instead of red error
“Media Center” renamed to “Image Pipeline” across all 5 pages
Gallery filter dropdown deduplicates properly — no more repeated product names
Gallery entries show Current (green) or Older (gray) badge per product
Upload flow deduplicates gallery entries by R2 key — re-uploads update instead of duplicating

title: Local Previews, Auto-Retry, Tooltip Hints & UX Renames
items: Instant image thumbnails via local canvas preview (canvas.toDataURL) — images appear immediately after upload, no CDN propagation delay
Auto-retry with backoff for failed processing jobs (30s → 60s → gives up) — skips auto-retry when credits are exhausted
Tooltip ? hint icons added to credits badge, Dismiss Done, and Retry Failed buttons — hover to see what each element does
“Processing Queue” tab renamed to “Upload Status”
“Clear Done” renamed to “Dismiss Done” — clarifies images stay in cloud storage
“Process All” renamed to “Retry Failed”
Gallery and queue thumbnails now prefer local preview URL over CDN URL for immediate display

title: Image Retry Engine, Credits Badge & Gallery Dedup
items: New multi-step image retry engine — gallery/queue thumbnails and preview popup now try: strip cache-bust → base URL → fresh cache-bust → then show fallback
Fixed duplicate gallery entries when using Process All — now updates existing entry instead of creating new one
Credits badge renamed from “remove.bg: 0 credits” to “BG Removal: 0 credits” with tooltip explaining the service

title: Gallery UX, Preview Retry, Design Consistency
items: Uploaded images now appear in Media Gallery immediately with “Original” badge — no more empty gallery after failed processing
Gallery entries update in-place when BG removal succeeds — no duplicates
Preview popup retries without cache-bust query params before showing error message
Media Center nav left-aligned — nav links now cluster near logo instead of floating center
Font stack standardized to “Segoe UI” across all pages (Workshop was using system font stack)
Content max-width standardized to 1200px on Changelog and Tasks pages
Padding and spacing normalized across all pages (24px 20px standard)
Changelog h1 reduced from 28px to 24px to match other pages

title: Preview Fix, Queue UX, Nav Standardization
items: Fixed broken image preview popup — now shows “Image could not be loaded” error message instead of broken icon
X (remove) button now available on stuck “Uploading” and “Processing” queue items — no more orphaned entries
Toast notification duration increased from 3s to 5s for better readability
All user-facing “R2” references replaced with “cloud storage” — friendlier terminology
Navigation fully standardized across Pipeline, Media Center, Workshop, Changelog, and Tasks — same order, same links, current page highlighted on every page

title: Workshop Card System Complete & Nav Sync
items: Workshop fully redesigned as card-based system — 6 phases with LED indicators matching Pipeline visual language
Detail panels with editable form fields, auto-save, and “Mark as Complete” verification for each card
Dynamic cards for Fleet (7 printers pre-populated), Materials, and Software phases — add/remove as needed
Machine Economics phase auto-calculates depreciation, maintenance, electricity, and blended hourly rate
Navigation now consistent across all 5 pages: Pipeline, Media Center, Workshop, Changelog, Tasks
Workshop version synced from v0.27.1 to v0.44.0

title: Gallery Cleanup, Task Tracker & UX Fixes
items: Gallery cards now show “Image unavailable” fallback instead of broken thumbnails
Hover any gallery card to reveal × remove button — cleans up junk entries
Added “Clear Gallery” and “Clear Done” buttons for bulk cleanup
New Task Tracker page — living backlog with priorities, milestones, and stats
Tasks link (purple) added to navigation on all pages

title: Auto-Process, File Validation & UX Polish
items: Images now auto-process immediately after upload — no manual “Process” button click needed
File type validation rejects non-image files (MP4, PDF, etc.) with a clear toast message
File browser dialog now only shows image file types
Queue “×” button now shows tooltip: “Remove from queue (image stays in R2)”
Action buttons reordered: delete left, primary action right
Navigation standardized across Pipeline, Media Center, and Changelog pages
Changelog CURRENT badge updated to reflect latest version

title: Image Preview Popup (No More New Tabs)
items: Clicking any image now opens a sleek overlay popup instead of a new browser tab
Media Center: queue thumbnails, View links, and gallery cards all use the popup
Pipeline: marketing bar thumb, modal image preview, and Image Fixer original/result all use the popup
Popup shows product label, close button (× or Escape), and “Open in new tab” link if needed
Click outside the image or press Escape to dismiss

title: Shared Product List (Single Source of Truth)
items: Pipeline now syncs product names to a shared opsflow_products localStorage key on every save and load
Media Center reads from that one shared key instead of digging through Pipeline internals
“+ Add” button also writes back to the shared list so products added from Media Center are visible everywhere
Replaces the 3-source fallback hack with a clean single source of truth — ready for a future database migration

title: Media Center Product Dropdown & Upload Fix
items: Product dropdown now pulls from 3 sources: Pipeline state, queue history, and gallery — no longer empty when visiting Media Center directly
Added “+ Add” button for manual product entry when no Pipeline products exist
Auto-selects the only product if there’s just one, so paste/drop/browse work immediately
Fixes paste and file browse being silently blocked when dropdown was empty

title: Auto-Crop & Center After Background Removal
items: Worker now sends crop: true to remove.bg API so returned images are tightly cropped around the product
Media Center adds a post-processing step: fetches the cropped image, scans for the product bounding box, and re-draws it centered on a clean 2000×2000 white canvas at ~90% fill
Both single Process and batch Process All jobs now auto-crop before finalizing
Products fill most of the frame instead of floating in excessive whitespace

title: Image Cache-Busting After Processing
items: R2 uploads now set Cache-Control: max-age=60 so Cloudflare edge doesn't cache stale images forever
After Media Center processes an image, URL gets ?v=timestamp to force browser refresh
Media Center updates Pipeline state with fresh URL so portfolio shows the processed version
Both single Process and Process All batch jobs now apply cache-busting

title: Media Center Product Selector
items: Added product selector dropdown to Media Center — must pick a product before uploading
Dropdown auto-populates from Pipeline product list, pre-selects last active product
Validates product selection on drag & drop, paste, and file browse

title: Media Center & Server-Side Background Removal
items: NEW: Media Center page with processing queue and media gallery
NEW: Server-side background removal via remove.bg API through Cloudflare Worker
NEW: /remove-bg Worker endpoint — fetches raw image from R2, sends to remove.bg, uploads clean version back
NEW: /credits Worker endpoint — check remaining remove.bg API credits
Pipeline popup now uploads raw image instantly, queues BG removal for Media Center
Removed browser-based @imgly/background-removal (CDN import issues) and coi-serviceworker
Drop zone supports drag & drop, clipboard paste, and file browse
Queue shows pending/processing/done/failed with Process All button
Media Gallery tab with per-product filtering
Added Media Center link to Pipeline navigation bar

title: Fix Self-Hosted Import Path
items: Changed import from index.js → index.mjs to match actual R2-hosted filename
Removed publicPath override — ONNX models load from IMG.LY default CDN at runtime
Handle both mod.default and mod.removeBackground exports for compatibility

title: Self-Hosted AI Background Removal
items: Self-host @imgly/background-removal library on R2 — eliminates CDN import failures
All WASM + ONNX model files served from images.opsflowbetter.com/libs/bgremoval/
Added publicPath config so AI models load from our own CDN, not third-party
Setup script (worker/setup-bgremoval.js) downloads and uploads library to R2
Retry button now imports from self-hosted URL instead of unpkg

title: AI Background Removal Fix & Image Display
items: Added cross-origin isolation service worker (coi-serviceworker.js) — enables SharedArrayBuffer required by the AI WASM engine on GitHub Pages
Switched AI library CDN from jsDelivr to unpkg for more reliable module loading
Fixed product image filling the entire browser window after Save — now capped at 200px height
Removed confusing “Use PhotoRoom” manual fallback text — replaced with cleaner retry guidance
Page will auto-reload once on first visit to activate the service worker (transparent to user)

title: Image Fixer Reliability & Clipboard Paste
items: R2 upload now shows visible error messages instead of failing silently
Inline R2 secret entry field replaces unreliable browser prompt() — themed to match the modal, with Enter key support
Console logging for R2 uploads (open DevTools → Console to debug if needed)
AI background removal timeout increased from 30s to 60s for slow connections
“Retry” link appears if AI model fails to load — re-imports module and retries without page reload
Clipboard paste (Ctrl+V) support in the Image Fixer — paste an image from Phone Link, screenshot tools, etc.
Drop zone text updated to show all three input methods: drag & drop, paste, or click to browse
Added .wrangler/ to .gitignore to keep Cloudflare cache out of the repo

title: R2 Auto-Upload — Image Fixer to Cloud Pipeline
items: Cloudflare Worker script for R2 image uploads with shared-secret auth and CORS support
Image Fixer now auto-uploads processed Amazon-ready JPEGs to R2 after processing — no manual upload step
Uploading again overwrites the existing image (R2 PUT semantics) — always latest version
Image URL auto-populated in state and modal fields after successful upload
R2 root auto-learned so future manual URL entries offer images.opsflowbetter.com in the dropdown
Graceful fallback: if R2 upload fails, download button appears with error details
Upload secret stored in localStorage (one-time prompt on first use)

title: Marketing Bar Cleanup & Button Relocation
items: Removed confusing color swatch dots and “3 colors” count from the marketing summary bar — variants label now reads “3 variants” in text only
Moved “Product Marketing” button from the summary bar to below the Overall Progress bar where it’s more prominent and uses existing empty space
Button now spans full width of the progress section for easy visibility
Marketing summary bar is now a slim, uncluttered status line with tagline excerpt and image thumbnail only

title: Product Marketing Modal
items: New “Product Marketing” modal consolidates tagline, image URL, Image Fixer, and color variants into one polished form
Replaced three separate bars (tagline, image URL, color variants) with a clean summary bar showing key data at a glance
Summary bar shows: tagline excerpt, image thumbnail, color swatches, and green “Product Marketing” button
Drag-and-drop image upload with animated progress bar (Loading AI → Analyzing → Removing background → Compositing → Done)
Native browser color picker for variant hex colors — click the swatch to pick any color visually
Inline variant editing: name, hex color, and per-variant image URL all editable in the modal
Modal closes on Escape key or clicking the backdrop
Image URL root dropdown with auto-parse preserved in modal
Live image preview as you type/paste the URL
Amazon specs shown inline below the Image Fixer drop zone

title: Built-In Image Fixer
items: New “Image Fixer” button on the product image bar — upload a raw photo for instant Amazon-ready processing
AI-powered background removal using @imgly/background-removal (runs entirely in browser, no server, no API key, no cost)
Auto-composites product on pure white (RGB 255,255,255) background at 2000x2000 pixels
Centers product at 85% fill ratio per Amazon requirements
Side-by-side original vs result preview with download button
Exports as JPEG at 95% quality — ready to upload to R2 bucket
Fallback mode: if AI model can’t load, resizes image on white background with link to PhotoRoom for external bg removal
~30MB AI model downloads on first use, then cached by browser for instant subsequent processing
Brightened grey text across all pages (#484f58 → #6e7681) for better readability

title: Color Variant Tracking
items: New colorVariants state field — each product stores an array of variants with name, hex color, and optional image URL
Variant chip UI in Pipeline product bar — visual swatches with color dots and labels
Click variant chip to edit (rename, change color, set image URL); right-click to remove
“+ Add” button for creating new variants with color name and hex code
Portfolio cards now display color swatches row when variants exist
Translucent/clear variants show checkered pattern swatch
Pre-populated: 45 RPM Adaptor (Matte Black, Stone Grey, Translucent Clear) and Improbability Spinner (Deep Space Blue, Nebula Purple, Glow Green)
Variants persist in localStorage, migrate on product rename, clean up on product remove

title: Smart Image URL & Portfolio Image Upgrade
items: Smart image URL input with root prefix dropdown — auto-learns URL roots from previous entries, stored in localStorage
Pick a saved root (e.g. images.opsflowbetter.com/) and just type the filename — no more pasting full URLs
Pasting a full URL auto-parses root and filename, saves root for future use
Display shows root in grey + filename in blue for easy scanning
Added “Specs” button with Amazon-ready image requirements panel (1600px min, white bg, 85% fill, JPEG, max 10MB)
Portfolio images now render on white background with object-fit:contain and 8px padding for professional display
Placeholder cards retain dark background when no image is set

title: Tagline & Image URL Persistence Fix
items: Fixed loadState() not restoring taglines and imageUrls from localStorage — data was lost on page reload
Added taglines and imageUrls to the state merge in loadState() alongside products, checklists, and gateSignatures

title: Phase 2 Data: 45 RPM Adaptor
items: G1 GO/NO-GO gate passed — Niche Viability Score 24/30, income math feasible at $12.99
P2.1 CAD Design — Fusion 360 model with full dimensional specs (38.1mm OD, 7.14mm spindle hole, knurled grip)
P2.2 Prototype & Iterate — 4 prototype versions tested on Audio-Technica, Crosley, and Pioneer turntables
P2.3 Lock Print Settings — Production slicer profile locked: 19 min/unit, 6.8g PETG-HF, 8-unit batch layout
G2 Design Lock gate passed — Phase 1 estimates validated with real data, viability score improved to 25/30
Default state updated: 45 RPM Adaptor now green through Phase 2 with full checklist completion
Welcome page version count updated to 42

title: Product Image URL & Portfolio Photos
items: New “Product image” URL field per product in the Pipeline — paste a link to any hosted image
Inline click-to-edit UI with thumbnail preview in the product bar area
Portfolio cards on the OpsFlowBetter homepage now display real product photos when an image URL is set, with graceful fallback to placeholder
Image URLs migrate correctly on product rename and are cleaned up on product removal
Future-proof architecture — URL field maps directly to a backend API image field with zero migration needed

title: Product Portfolio Taglines
items: New “Portfolio tagline” field on each product in the Pipeline — a short marketing line that describes the product for prospects
Click-to-edit inline UI below the product bar, with Enter to save and Escape to cancel
Taglines auto-populate into the Product Portfolio cards on the OpsFlowBetter homepage via the existing data bridge
Taglines migrate correctly on product rename and are cleaned up on product removal
Tagline editing respects the read-only edit lock

title: Product Portfolio Showcase
items: Elevated Product Portfolio to its own dedicated section on the OpsFlowBetter homepage — “Built with our own tools. Tracked in real time.”
“Our Products” nav link added to homepage top nav, Pipeline header, and Workshop header (green accent, links to /#portfolio)
Portfolio section now hidden until products exist in Pipeline, then renders as a proper marketing showcase with auto-populated status badges and progress bars

title: Read-Only Edit Lock
items: Password-protected edit lock across Pipeline and Workshop — read-only by default, visitors can browse but not modify data
Lock icon in header toggles between locked (read-only) and unlocked (edit mode)
All state-modifying actions guarded: LED cycling, Mark as Complete, gate decisions, product add/remove/rename, card verify/unverify, field editing, reset
Workshop fields disabled when locked — inputs greyed out to prevent accidental edits
Shared lock state between Pipeline and Workshop via localStorage — unlock once, edit everywhere
Toast notifications inform visitors they’re in read-only mode when they attempt changes

title: Collapsible Workshop Phases
items: Workshop phase columns are now collapsible — click the phase header to toggle cards open/closed
Collapse arrow indicator rotates to show state (down = expanded, right = collapsed)
Collapsed state persists across sessions via localStorage
Improves mobile UX by letting users focus on one phase at a time

title: Product Portfolio + OpsFlowBetter Page Audit
items: Product Portfolio on OpsFlowBetter homepage — auto-populated from Pipeline localStorage, shows product name, status badge, progress bar, and phase dots
Status badges derived from Pipeline progress: In Development → Design Locked → Production Ready → Coming Soon → Live on Amazon
Placeholder product images with camera icon — ready for real photography
OpsFlowBetter index.html: updated P2M description (31 steps), removed "Workshop Ops" from Coming Soon (it's live!), fixed changelog link, added cache-bust meta tags, added Workshop Setup button

P2M welcome.html: updated stats (31 steps, 35 versions), fixed Phase 3/4/5 step tags, added Workshop & Data Bridge features, updated roadmap to reflect shipped features, fixed footer version from v0.9.0 to v0.26.0

title: Workshop → Pipeline Data Bridge + Workshop Enhancements
items: Workshop → Pipeline data bridge — Pipeline reads Workshop localStorage to display blended rate, material costs, packaging, fleet, and labor data in relevant step detail panels (P1.5, P2.1, P2.3, P3.1, P3.2)
Blended Rate summary banner in Workshop header — prominently displays the all-in $/hr rate with component breakdown (Dep + Maint + Elec)
W3.5 Packaging Defaults card — poly bag, FNSKU label, insert card costs with auto-calculated total per unit
Hours per Machine per Year now editable in W3.2 Maintenance (was hardcoded at 800) — feeds maintenance-per-hour calculation
Bridge-disconnected hint on data-dependent Pipeline steps when Workshop not yet configured

title: Pipeline Completion Summary Bar
items: Completion summary bar in Pipeline product bar — shows percentage, step count, and overall LED
Matches Workshop header pattern for visual consistency across pages

title: Mark as Complete Button & P/W Prefixes
items: Mark as Complete button on all Pipeline step detail panels (non-gate)
Undo Completion button when step is green — reverts to yellow with 5-second undo toast
Pipeline step numbers prefixed with “P” (P1.1, P2.1, etc.) for cross-page clarity
Workshop card numbers prefixed with “W” (W1.1, W3.2, etc.) for cross-page clarity
Gates remain G1, G2, G5 — no prefix needed

title: Workshop Audit — Data Accuracy & UX Overhaul
items: Labor 5.1 restructured: Owner role with labor absorption decision (was generic operator at $12/hr)
H2D max speed corrected from 500 to 300mm/s
Purchase price and wattage fields added to all Fleet cards
Phase 3 depreciation and electricity now auto-calculate from Fleet data (no manual entry)
All alert() popups replaced with toast notifications
All confirm() dialogs replaced with instant action + 5-second undo toast
Reset State button with undo toast (matches Pipeline pattern)

title: Undo Toast for Revise Decision
items: Revise Decision now clears instantly — no more confirm popup
5-second undo toast with one-click restore of previous signature and decision
Previous decision state snapshot enables clean undo without data loss

title: NO-GO Checklist Fix
items: NO-GO now locks “Decision made” and “Decision documented with date” checkboxes
hasDecision checks for signature existence instead of LED color — covers all three decision types

title: Gate UX Fixes
items: Detail panel stays open after GO, Revisit Later, and NO-GO decisions
Detail panel stays open after LED cycle and Revise Decision
Removed NO-GO confirm dialog — decision records immediately

title: localStorage Persistence
items: All state persists across browser refreshes via localStorage
Auto-saves on every LED cycle, checkbox toggle, gate decision, product add/remove/rename
Reset button in product bar to restore defaults with confirmation
State loads from localStorage on page init, falls back to hardcoded defaults

title: Gate Celebration & LED Colors
items: Gate LED colors on pipeline cards — red, yellow, green now render correctly
“BOOM Baby!” celebration popup on GO decision with auto-dismiss
Detail panel stays open after GO click so user sees locked checkboxes and signature

title: Soft-Lock Gate Decisions
items: Gate decisions soft-lock after button click — buttons replaced by locked signature block
“Revise Decision” link with confirm dialog to re-open gate for new decision
Revising clears signature, resets gate to red, and re-shows decision buttons

title: Automation-Only Checklist Items
items: Gate checklist now has three states: locked (green + lock), disabled/auto (greyed + “auto” tag), and user-checkable
“Decision made” and “Decision documented with date” are disabled until a decision button is clicked
New “auto” badge on automation-only items so users know they can’t manually check them

title: Gate Electronic Signature
items: Electronic signature block on gate decisions — records name, decision, and timestamp
Signature auto-populates and locks when GO, Revisit Later, or NO-GO is clicked
Color-coded decision label (green GO, yellow Revisit, red NO-GO) with lock icon

title: Fix Detail Panel Click
items: Fixed regression: clicking pipeline cards threw ReferenceError (steps → stepDefinitions) breaking detail panel

title: Auto-Lock Checklists
items: Checklist items auto-check and lock (green + lock icon) when step LED is green
G1 gate checklist wired to gate decision state — items lock based on actual criteria evaluation and decision
getAutoLockedChecklist() function handles step-level and gate-level lock logic
Locked checkboxes are disabled with green accent color and lock emoji indicator

title: Gate Buttons & Spacing
items: Gate decision buttons moved to bottom of detail panel for natural UI flow
Pipeline vertical spacing tightened — Phase 1 fits on screen without scrolling

title: Gate Decision UX
items: Gate cards now show G1, G2, G5 labels matching step numbering pattern
GO / NO-GO / Revisit Later decision buttons on gate detail panels
Gate LED no longer cycles on click — decisions made via buttons only
Gate status message shows current decision and what it unlocks
Cache-busting meta tags on all pages — browser always loads latest after deploy

title: Workshop Card-Based Redesign
items: Workshop redesigned as card-based system matching Pipeline visual language — 6 phases, 20+ cards, LED status indicators
Phase 1: Facility & Utilities (electricity rate, workspace, internet/services)
Phase 2: Printer Fleet with dynamic add/remove cards (7 printers pre-populated)
Phase 3: Machine Economics with auto-calculated blended hourly rate from depreciation + maintenance + electricity
Phase 4: Materials Library with dynamic cards, auto-calculated cost per gram
Phase 5: Labor (primary operator + optional additional labor)
Phase 6: Software & Tools with dynamic add/remove cards
Slide-in detail panel with data entry forms, purpose descriptions, and "Mark as Complete" verification
localStorage persistence — all Workshop data survives page reload

Phase progress bars showing per-phase completion
Steps 1.4 and 1.5 LEDs set to GREEN for 45 RPM Adaptor

title: Workshop Setup + Income Target Math
items: Workshop Setup page (workshop.html) — standalone page defining printer fleet (7 machines), machine rates ($0.35/hr blended), materials inventory, labor rates, and software stack
Workshop Summary Card — at-a-glance view of active printers, daily print hours, blended rate, materials stocked, and operator rate
Editable tables with add/remove rows for printers, materials, and software
Step 1.5 (Income Target Math) for 45 RPM Adaptor — backward math from $2k/month: 154 units/month, 5.1/day, ~29% net margin, one printer sufficient. Three price scenarios ($9.99-$14.99) all feasible.
Full three-layer data model for Step 1.5: Analysis (7 sections), Evidence (4 tables), Automation Roadmap (5 gaps)
Pipeline header now includes Workshop link alongside About P2M and Changelog
Welcome page nav updated with Workshop link
Step 1.5 references softened from hardcoded "Bambu X1C" to "see Workshop Setup" for fleet/rate data
Step 1.5 LED set to YELLOW for 45 RPM Adaptor

title: Phase-Gated Locking + Step Descriptions
items: Phase-gated card locking — future phases are locked (grey LED, grey border, lock icon) until prerequisite gate or phase steps are completed
Lock rules: Phase 1 always open. Phase 2 unlocks at G1 green. Phase 3 at G2 green. Phases 4/5/7 unlock when all prior phase steps are green. Phase 6 at G5 green.
Locked card preview — clicking a locked card still opens the detail panel with a lock banner explaining what to complete, read-only checklists, and disabled LED cycling
Lock icon (🔒) displayed on locked step cards and gates in both desktop and mobile views
Expanded all 30+ step descriptions with outcome expectations — every step now tells the user exactly what they'll "walk away with" after completing it
Phase 2 step purposes (2.1, 2.2, 2.3) updated to reference G2 gate validation of Phase 1 estimates
Changelog link simplified from "v0.11.0 Changelog" to "Changelog" — version now shown in header subtitle
Red LED cards now show matching red left border (was transparent)

title: Niche Viability Score + Flow Integrity
items: Step 1.4 (Niche Viability Score) for 45 RPM Adaptor — 6-factor scoring with Summary, Analysis, Evidence, and Automation Roadmap layers. Score: 25/30 STRONG GO

Phase 1 flow integrity cleanup — removed forward-looking data from Steps 1.1-1.4 that assumed prior product knowledge (specific tolerances, print times, three-model strategy). Data now reflects what a first-timer would know at each step.
Step 1.1 Unique Angle simplified to hypothesis ("better durability and precision") instead of pre-defining variant models
Steps 1.2/1.3 analysis sections reworded from certainties to hypotheses (e.g., "should deliver" instead of "delivers", specs "TBD in Phase 2")
Step 1.4 3D Print Feasibility scored as 4 (estimated) instead of 5 — exact print time, material weight, and tolerances deferred to Phase 2
G2 Design Lock gate upgraded — now includes checklist items to revisit Step 1.4 scores with confirmed Phase 2 data before proceeding to Phase 3
Detail panel toggle — clicking an already-open step card closes the panel (re-click to dismiss)
Remove product functionality — red × button on tabs with confirmation modal, prevents removing last product
Step 1.3 flipped to GREEN for 45 RPM Adaptor
Step 1.4 set to YELLOW for 45 RPM Adaptor (data populated, under review)
